title: Syracuse’s e-scooters are popular with young riders, a worry the company says it’s trying to fix
summary: Veo, operator of Syracuse's e-scooter and e-bike sharing system, is collaborating with city lawmakers to address safety concerns regarding underage riders. The initiative focuses on age verification and usage restrictions to limit access by minors, indicating both regulatory engagement and corporate responsibility efforts.

political_signal.description: City lawmakers and Veo executive jointly addressing age-access concerns for e-scooter/e-bike program; indicates regulatory oversight and potential policy adjustments to usage restrictions

processing_notes: No specific department assigned; likely involves Parks or Transportation given micromobility focus
